channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian shares rise on copper miner gains and Middle East tensions
item.description: Australian shares broke a four-day losing streak as investors returned to mining and financial sectors following renewed Middle East tensions that pushed oil prices higher. The S&P/ASX 200 Index gaine...

item.title: Heartstopper's Final Chapter: Actors Reflect on Queer Representation and Character Farewell
item.description: The article discusses the conclusion of the popular TV series 'Heartstopper,' focusing on the perspectives of lead actors Kit Connor and Joe Locke. It highlights their reflections on portraying queer ...

item.title: Jammu and Kashmir Schools Ordered to Review Books for Pro-Separatist Content Amid Controversy
item.description: The Jammu and Kashmir government has directed all government and private schools, along with coaching centres, to review all books for 'inappropriate or objectionable content'. This directive follows ...

item.title: Christa Vogel: The Iconic Model of 1950s and 1960s Fashion
item.description: Christa Vogel was a German fashion model who became one of the most elegant and recognizable faces in international fashion photography during the late 1950s and early 1960s. Her refined beauty, grace...

item.title: Residential fire damages property in Northeast Calgary
item.description: A residential fire has caused damage to a home in Northeast Calgary, prompting local authorities to investigate the incident. The blaze, which occurred on July 9, 2026, was reported around 11:32 PM ED...

item.title: Zsa Zsa Padilla pays tribute to Dolphy on his death anniversary
item.description: Zsa Zsa Padilla recently honored the memory of her late colleague and friend, Dolphy, on the anniversary of his passing. The tribute comes as part of ongoing efforts to celebrate his legacy in Philipp...

item.title: World Bank Funds $375.9m to Modernize Pakistan's Power Grid and Boost Renewable Energy
item.description: The World Bank has approved $375.9 million in financing for Pakistan's Grid Stability Enhancement Project under the BEST-PAK Multiphase Programmatic Approach. This initiative aims to modernize Pakista...

item.title: Bafana Bafana's World Cup Performance Sparks Hope for Future Success
item.description: South Africa's national football team, Bafana Bafana, made history by advancing past the group stage at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, marking their first-ever progression in the tournament. Captain Ronwen ...

item.title: Baguio City Commits to Annual West Philippine Sea Awareness Campaign
item.description: Baguio City has officially declared July 12 as West Philippine Sea Awareness Day to support national efforts in raising public awareness about the country's maritime rights. Mayor Benjamin Magalong ap...

item.title: Ruth Ellis, the last woman hanged in Britain, receives posthumous pardon after 71 years
item.description: Ruth Ellis, the last woman executed for murder in the United Kingdom, has been granted a posthumous pardon 71 years after her death. The pardon, announced by Justice Secretary David Lammy, acknowledge...

item.title: UAG Coalition Still Finalizing Presidential Candidate Selection for 2027 Elections
item.description: Martha Karua, co-convenor of the United Alternative Government (UAG), has clarified that the coalition has not yet decided on a presidential candidate for the 2027 elections. She emphasized that the f...

item.title: Big Splash Reopening Faces Uncertainty Amid Funding Challenges

item.title: Kenya's Political and Economic Challenges Amid Protests
item.description: Kenya is facing significant challenges as recurring street protests, known as 'maandamano,' continue to disrupt daily life and raise concerns about political stability and economic growth. These demon...

item.title: Government begins hydrogen mobility pilot projects on 10 highway routes as part of clean transport push
item.description: Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways Nitin Gadkari has said that hydrogen is expected to play a major role in the future of India's transportation sector, with the government already conduct...

item.title: Sault Transit Revamp Sparks Rider Backlash
item.description: The proposed transit revamp in Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario, has sparked significant backlash from riders, raising concerns about service disruptions and potential impacts on daily commutes. The initiati...

item.title: Islamic Scholars Rule Cryptocurrency Transactions Impermissible Under Sharia Law
item.description: A fatwa issued by Mufti Muhammad Taqi Usmani, president of Wifaq-ul-Madaris Al-Arabia Pakistan and Darul Uloom Karachi, has declared that purchasing goods with cryptocurrency is not permissible under ...

item.title: Morocco Coach Mohamed Ouahbi Acknowledges France's Victory in 2026 World Cup
item.description: Morocco's head coach, Mohamed Ouahbi, admitted France deserved their 2-0 win after the Atlas Lions were eliminated from the 2026 FIFA World Cup quarter-finals. The match, held at Boston Stadium on Jul...

item.title: Tacloban School Resumes Classes Amid Lingering Trauma From Shooting Incident
item.description: San Jose National High School in Tacloban City reopened classes on July 6, 2026, two weeks after a fatal shooting on June 22 that killed three students and injured 20 others. The resumption of classes...

item.title: Giants pitcher Logan Webb deletes X account after social media outburst over criticism and poor performance
item.description: San Francisco Giants pitcher Logan Webb faced intense backlash after deleting his X account following a contentious online exchange with critics. The incident occurred after the Giants suffered a 10-0...

item.title: US and Iran escalate military strikes amid escalating tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran have escalated military strikes for a second consecutive day, following President Donald Trump's declaration that an interim peace deal was 'over'. Iran accused the US of at...
